notes: Available public evidence located through WHO and CDC supports a narrow estimate about adolescent tobacco-use prevalence and the existence of public surveillance infrastructure in Asia, especially the WHO South-East Asia Region. The strongest directly reproducible figures found here indicate that WHO reported about 11 million adolescent tobacco users aged 13-15 years in the South-East Asia Region, representing about 30% of the global total, with an average current tobacco-use prevalence of about 11% based on surveys conducted during 2012-2022 and applied to 2021 populations. Older CDC/WHO GYTS reporting for 1999-2005 reported South-East Asian regional prevalence around 12.9% for any tobacco use and 4.3% for cigarette smoking among students aged 13-15 years. CDC also documents a standing public data system covering more than 20 tobacco-data topics via GTSSData.
